[Table of Contents](https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/toc)

![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69DD569682238CF8EC0621E2/69DD569982238CF8EC0621E9/en_US/89871c0fe9a98c4bbe05d2665cfe3180.png)

# Set sleep and wake settings for your Mac

You can set sleep and wake settings for your Mac. For example, you can set your Mac to go to sleep after a specified duration of inactivity, or set it to wake for network access. Keep in mind that delaying, preventing, or allowing disruption of sleep may increase power consumption.

_Note:_ Some of the options may not be available, depending on your Mac.

## Set your Mac to go to sleep after inactivity

1. On your Mac, choose Apple menu ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69DD569682238CF8EC0621E2/69DD569982238CF8EC0621E9/en_US/2f77cc85238452e25cb517130188bf99.png) \> System Settings, then click Lock Screen ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69DD569682238CF8EC0621E2/69DD569982238CF8EC0621E9/en_US/96073ab1b294ffc6b64fdbfbfdc70334.png) in the sidebar. (You may need to scroll down.)

[Open Lock Screen settings for me](x-help-action://openPrefPane?bundleId=com.apple.Lock-Screen-Settings.extension)

2. Do any of the following:

   - Click the pop-up menu next to “Turn display off on battery when inactive,” then choose an option.

   - Click the pop-up menu next to “Turn display off on power adapter when inactive,” then choose an option.

## Specify sleep and wake settings for a Mac laptop

1. On your Mac, choose Apple menu ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69DD569682238CF8EC0621E2/69DD569982238CF8EC0621E9/en_US/2f77cc85238452e25cb517130188bf99.png) \> System Settings, then click Battery ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69DD569682238CF8EC0621E2/69DD569982238CF8EC0621E9/en_US/89871c0fe9a98c4bbe05d2665cfe3180.png) in the sidebar. (You may need to scroll down.)

[Open Battery settings for me](x-help-action://openPrefPane?bundleId=com.apple.Battery-Settings.extension*BatteryPreferences)

2. Click Options, then do any of the following:

   - _Stop automatic sleeping when the display is off:_ Turn on “Prevent automatic sleeping on power adapter when the display is off.”

   - _Put hard disks to sleep:_ Turn on “Put hard disks to sleep when possible.”

   - _Update your system while it’s asleep:_ Click the pop-up menu next to Enable Power Nap, then choose an option.

     See [Turn Power Nap on or off on Mac](https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/turn-power-nap-on-or-off-mh40774/26/mac/26).

   - _Allow your Mac to wake briefly so users can access shared services (if applicable):_ Click the pop-up menu next to “Wake for network access,” then choose an option.

     See [Share your Mac resources when it’s in sleep](https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/share-your-mac-resources-when-its-in-sleep-mh27905/26/mac/26).

## Specify sleep and wake settings for a Mac desktop computer

1. On your Mac, choose Apple menu ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69DD569682238CF8EC0621E2/69DD569982238CF8EC0621E9/en_US/2f77cc85238452e25cb517130188bf99.png) \> System Settings, then click Energy ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69DD569682238CF8EC0621E2/69DD569982238CF8EC0621E9/en_US/cc0cceb27816aad8312a1e7f9ee76541.png) in the sidebar. (You may need to scroll down.)

[Open Energy settings for me](x-help-action://openPrefPane?bundleId=com.apple.Battery-Settings.extension)

2. Do any of the following:

   - _Stop automatic sleeping when the display is off:_ Turn on “Prevent automatic sleeping when the display is off.”

   - _Put hard disks to sleep:_ Turn on “Put hard disks to sleep when possible.”

   - _Update your system while it’s asleep:_ Turn on Enable Power Nap.

     See [Turn Power Nap on or off on Mac](https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/turn-power-nap-on-or-off-mh40774/26/mac/26).

   - _Allow your Mac to wake briefly so users can access shared services (if applicable):_ Turn on “Wake for network access.”

     See [Share your Mac resources when it’s in sleep](https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/share-your-mac-resources-when-its-in-sleep-mh27905/26/mac/26).

**See also** [Put your Mac to sleep or wake it](https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/put-your-mac-to-sleep-or-wake-it-mh10330/26/mac/26) [Save energy on your Mac](https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/optimize-your-mac-battery-life-mh35848/26/mac/26) [Change Energy settings on a Mac desktop computer](https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/change-energy-settings-mchlp1168/26/mac/26)

web.title: KeepingYouAwake: Prevents your Mac from going to sleep
web.description: Features. prevents your Mac from going to sleep with one click; pre-defined activation durations; can disable itself when the battery level is low ...
web.markdown:
![](https://keepingyouawake.app/screenshot@2x.a4259f34.jpg)

### [Download](https://github.com/newmarcel/KeepingYouAwake/releases/download/1.6.8/KeepingYouAwake-1.6.8.zip)

[Latest Release · 1.6.8](https://github.com/newmarcel/KeepingYouAwake/releases/latest)

### [Source Code](https://github.com/newmarcel/KeepingYouAwake)

[Changelog](https://github.com/newmarcel/KeepingYouAwake/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md) · [Wiki](https://github.com/newmarcel/KeepingYouAwake/wiki) · [Issues](https://github.com/newmarcel/KeepingYouAwake/issues)

## Features

- prevents your Mac from going to sleep with one click
- pre-defined activation durations
- can disable itself when the battery level is low
- supports Retina displays and Dark Mode
- supports macOS High Sierra 10.13 and newer
- translated into various languages by the community

* * *

web.title: Caffeine - Keeps your Mac awake.
web.markdown:
[Tahoe](https://www.caffeine-app.net/en/#)

- [Tahoe](https://www.caffeine-app.net/en/?macos=tahoe)
- [Sequoia](https://www.caffeine-app.net/en/?macos=sequoia)
- [Sonoma](https://www.caffeine-app.net/en/?macos=sonoma)
- [Ventura](https://www.caffeine-app.net/en/?macos=ventura)
- [Monterey](https://www.caffeine-app.net/en/?macos=monterey)

# Caffeine

Keeps your Mac awake.

[![Caffeine app icon](https://www.caffeine-app.net/images/tahoe/caffeine-2x.png)\\
Download NowVersion 1.6.4 - 4.5 MB](https://www.caffeine-app.net/download/tahoe/)

Caffeine is a tiny open source program that puts an icon in the right side of your menubar. Click it to prevent your Mac from automatically going to sleep, dimming the screen or starting screen savers.

### Usage

![Caffeine icon in the macOS menu bar](https://www.caffeine-app.net/images/tahoe/menubar.png)

Caffeine puts a coffee cup icon in the right side of your menu bar. Click the cup to toggle whether Caffeine is active or not -- a full cup means Caffeine will prevent your Mac from automatically going to sleep, dimming the screen or starting screen savers. An empty cup means your Mac will sleep normally.

For more control, right-click (or ⌘-click) the icon to show the menu. From here, you can access the preferences window or set a timeout if you only need Caffeine to prevent sleep for a little while.

![Caffeine dropdown menu showing duration options](https://www.caffeine-app.net/images/tahoe/menu.png)

![Caffeine preferences window with startup and timer settings](https://www.caffeine-app.net/images/tahoe/preferences.png)

Caffeine is intended to be simple, yet powerful. Options you can configure include whether to start Caffeine automatically every time you start up your Mac, whether Caffeine should activate every time it starts, and a default duration if you always want Caffeine to turn itself off after a set time.

### FAQ

- #### Is this the same Caffeine that I've used before?


Yes! Tomas Franzén of Lighthead Software originally developed Caffeine in 2006, and it has been a well known and loved utility for Mac users for many years. Its simplicity has allowed it to continue working perfectly long after active development had ceased.


In 2018, Michael Jones (IntelliScape Computer Solutions) reached out to Tomas to inquire if they could continue development of Caffeine.


Tomas has graciously provided the source code under an open source license, allowing IntelliScape Computer Solutions to continue developing Caffeine where he left off.


In 2022 we started modernizing the codebase and added support for macOS 11 and above. We also translated the application into several languages.

- #### Does this work with macOS 10.x?


No, this version requires at least macOS 11 (Big Sur). Caffeine for macOS Yosemite or later (including Catalina) is available at: [https://www.intelliscapesolutions.com/apps/caffeine/](https://www.intelliscapesolutions.com/apps/caffeine/)
- #### How is Caffeine different or better than alternatives (such as Amphetamine, KeepingYouAwake, etc)?


Due to the long period of inactivity for Caffeine, a lot of different and great options have been developed. While the alternatives are great apps and definitely worth your consideration, we believe that Caffeine's power lies in its simplicity and ease of use.


### Support

If you have questions, comments or other feedback just [get in touch.](https://www.caffeine-app.net/en/contact/)

### Source Code

_Caffeine_ is open source software. It's licensed under MIT license and the source code is available on [Github](https://github.com/domzilla/Caffeine).

## More apps you'll love

Purpose-built macOS utilities crafted with the same attention to detail.

# **Sick of Your Mac Going to Sleep? This Free Mac App Keeps it Awake**

## Simple? Clean? Open source? What’s not to love?

[![Michael Swengel](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:32:32/1*sJX8bt9RrXTUdqViwUFowg.jpeg)](https://medium.com/@michaelswengel?source=post_page---byline--6be36c4cdd0a---------------------------------------)

[Michael Swengel](https://medium.com/@michaelswengel?source=post_page---byline--6be36c4cdd0a---------------------------------------)

Follow

7 min read

·

Apr 7, 2026

48

[Listen](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fplans%3Fdimension%3Dpost_audio_button%26postId%3D6be36c4cdd0a&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2F%40michaelswengel%2Fsick-of-your-mac-going-to-sleep-this-free-mac-app-keeps-it-awake-6be36c4cdd0a&source=---header_actions--6be36c4cdd0a---------------------post_audio_button------------------)

Share

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/0*L1VPL-PV3USBFjka)

Photo by [Maria Freyenbacher](https://unsplash.com/@freyenbacher?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral) on [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral)

I hit upload on a new YouTube video, and walked away. I was excited to see the video live on YouTube soon, but I had to leave the house for a bit. I figured the upload would finish without me, and I’d see the video online from my phone in an hour or so.

But hours passed, and still… nothing? Weird. Was something wrong with YouTube? Was something wrong with my channel?

Nope. Neither, actually.

I got home, and had a frustrating realization: My Mac had gone to sleep in the middle of the upload, so it didn’t finish. That means the video I was excited to go live? Didn’t. Cool.

What I didn’t realize at the time is that macOS is set to automatically sleep. (I know. Rookie move.) Makes sense, but massively inconvenient if I need to step away and don’t have my computer plugged in.

From that day forward, I wanted a fix to keep this from happening again, and I found a few possible solutions.

One is a great little app that I think you’ll like.

## The Settings way

It took me way to long to realize (longer than I’d like to admit) that in the macOS Settings app…

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*xnuf4dxCVlpZHCsh9FPqXQ.png)

Screenshot provided by the author.

This setting will allow macOS to still turn off the display, which is a good power saving feature, especially for laptops, without letting the computer itself go to sleep.

That means if you have a download running or a render… rendering, it won’t be interrupted by macOS trying to save a few watts.

This is a good setting to enable if you frequently work with a power adapter attached, and you primarily run those operations that you don’t want interrupted while you’re plugged in to power.

However, this does _not_ help if you’re running on battery power. Even with this turned on, your Mac will still go to…
